strBody: Ostranauts takes place in 2079, after an ablation cascade rendered Earth's orbit too dangerous to traverse.

In 2035 the research expedition Nereus-3 discovered the first extraterrestrial life in the System: a biomarine ecosystem of long, thin sea creatures populating an enormous thermal vent deep below the frozen oceans of Europa.

The beings were named Lusca.
After the Ganymede Coup, the Confederacy established a blockade at Porto Nuevo designed to sanction offworld trade with Atlantis, the submarine colony beneath the frozen oceans of Europa.
Because the oceans of Europa are so deep, the Atlantis submarine colony consists of a series of modular habitats anchored to ice sheets sometimes hundreds of kilometers above the sea floor.

Fort Simpson was established in 2054 after the Ganymede Coup and represents the capital colony of The Galilean Confederacy.

While the revolutionary American government maintains control of the region, it remains fractured into dozens of settlements, many of which still claim sovereignty.

The Hangzhou Orbital (Hangzhou Gong, 杭州宫) is an O'Neill Cylinder space habitat located at Mars' 5th Lagrange point.

The colony is designed to resemble, and even improve on, Earth environments, from its near 1G spin gravity, its 24-hour Earth day, and its sustainable, symbiotic ecosystem.

Permanent work visas to Hangzhou Orbital were originally created to grant entry to engineers and construction workers for station maintenance.

In practice, however, they are used for indentured servitude, as the living standards are so high on the colony that demand for residency far outweighs the supply.

The West Lake region of the Martian Hangzhou Orbital is — bar none — the most expensive real estate in the System.

The median sale value for a house on West Lake has remained at nearly two hundred times the average yearly salary of an apprenticed technician, or a junior non-commissioned officer in the Tharsis Navy.

A sign of great status in the highly coveted West Lake region of the Martian Hangzhou Orbital is owning a private jetty extending into the lake itself so that one may fish.

The law there is as it is in the rest of the System: what you can catch, you keep.

Jade Rabbit (Yutu, 玉兔) on Earth's moon Luna is the oldest offworld colony in the System besides the International Space Station. It began as a research base established in 2021, shortly after the completion of China's space elevator.

It was named for the Chinese lunar rover, which was itself named for the pet rabbit of Chang'e, the goddess of the Moon in Chinese mythology.

Much of the infrastructure on Earth's moon Luna was left unfinished and abandoned as business interests left for Mars and the Belt.

Workers walked off excavation sites mid-development, leaving wiring exposed, enormous mining drones idle or even actively digging, processed meals and card games lying in tableaux to gather dust, preserved by vacuum.
The Hotel Mare Imbrium on Earth's abandoned moon Luna was a thriving site of space tourism before it experienced several freak, high-profile episodes of mass psychosis and death.

K-Leg station was established in 2032 as a stopover point for Ayotimiwa, a Nigerian shipping company.

The settlement consisted of five structures: a refueling station, a fuel and supply dump, a bubble dormitory, and a bar called Mescaform. Of the original settlement, only the refueling station and the bar remain.

New California, Newcal, is the de facto capital of Venus's aerostat colonies, and the only one large enough to be considered a metropolitan area.

The city is a modern aerostat designed after the first wave of experimental habitats in Venus's upper atmosphere.

The Venusian aerostat Newcal declared its independence from the Old United States in 2062 after the NCN Stevie Nicks, while attempting to make contact with the US government on Earth, was destroyed by automated defences in Low Earth Orbit.

Surviving astronauts reported failure to raise USNORTHCOM on comms multiple times before the attack. It remains the position of the Newcal government that the US command structure has been compromised.

Using small, fast, fixed-wing attack craft and hit & run tactics, the pirates of Venus are able to force anything less than heavily guarded shipments into an unpleasant choice: cede control of the shipment to the pirates, or be 'sunk', forced down into the crushing atmosphere at Venus's surface.

The Venusian aerostat Newcal suffers from piracy directed at shipments of resources and new colonists.

Striking from a hidden base, pirates hit the major atmo-to-orbit transfers and local Venus trade.

Other aerostat colonies afflicted include Encantado, an early Brazilian colony, and Cloudbreak, owned and run by a contract research organisation of the same name researching the Venusian surface.

Voltaire is a network of research facilities on Mars' moon Deimos, which is owned and run by Chinese technology corporation Brave New World.

Besides maintaining the facilities and conducting its own research, BNW rents research spaces, services them for other technology giants including Renbao and Zhuangzi, manufactures high-tech electronic components, and supports the private university, the Voltaire University of Science and Technology.

Virginia is the capital city of Titan, the moon of Saturn. An offworld territory of the United States, Titan is known for its technological superiority, the abundance of its natural resources, and its aggressive foreign policy.

Titan's growth throughout the colony rush is popularly attributed to a nationwide reaffirmation of American exceptionalism, and the realisation of an intergenerational promise of space travel particular to Americans since the first Moon landing.

A double digit growth of the US GDP in 2032 was linked to the promise of the space colony, as well as then-recent revival of rust belt states like Michigan.

Titan ships and habitats are state of the art, and the wealth of scientific and engineering acumen on Virginia and other Titan-controlled colonies exceeded the rest of the System until very recently.

The United States military-industrial complex - multinational military contractors like Weber Tools & Oil and Smartlink, as well as NASA's Outer Space Command - made Titan its offworld headquarters.
There are rumours of a black market trader buying and selling ship parts for better prices than the port kiosks, somewhere on OKLG station.

Harbourmaster Adeyemi vehemently denies the existence of a black market trade on OKLG.

"I run a tight ship. The only rats here are the ones I keep as pets".
